The word level is a palindrome that reads the same forward and backward.  
We will design a MySQL stored procedure to handle the deletion of inactive users safely.  
First, the procedure will start a transaction to ensure data consistency throughout the process.  
It will then insert a record into the status_log table for each user before deletion occurs.  
The insertion will capture the user_id, the current status, and the current modification_date.  
Next, the procedure will delete all rows from the users table where the status equals Inactive.  
After the deletion, the transaction will be committed if no errors are encountered during execution.  
If any error arises, the transaction will be rolled back to preserve the original data state.  
The error handling section will use a DECLARE EXIT HANDLER to catch and report specific issues.  
This approach ensures that the total number of affected rows is tracked accurately in the log.  
The procedure can be invoked at any time to clean up inactive accounts without manual intervention.  
Below is the complete SQL code implementing these requirements for your database environment.  
The total count of deleted records is logged for audit and future reference purposes.
